title: Atalaya annularis
taxonomy: Atalaya annularis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a type of shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is further classified as a member of the genus Atalaya, which consists of several species of shrubs and small trees. Atalaya annularis is believed to be closely related to other species within the Rubiaceae family. Further classification and research may be needed to fully understand the plant's taxonomic position within the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Atalaya annularis, also known as Atalaya or Salsola, is a shrub-like plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Atalaya annularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Atalaya annularis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also consumed as a food ingredient in some traditional dishes.
